matches.x: A Song of degrees. Behold, bless ye the LORD, all [ye] servants of the LORD, which by night stand in the house of the LORD.

4 This is the way of Torah: A morsel with salt thou shalt eat, ‘and thou shalt drink water by measure’; and thou shalt sleep on the ground, and thou shalt live a life of hardship, and thou shalt labour in the Torah. If thou doest thus ‘happy shalt thou be, and it shall be well with thee’; Ps. ‘happy ’ in this world, and ‘ well with thee’ in the world to come.
[There is] nothing better for a man, [than] that he should eat and drink, and [that] he should make his soul enjoy good in his labour. This also I saw, that it [was] from the hand of God.
And labour, working with our own hands: being reviled, we bless; being persecuted, we suffer it:
Let the elders that rule well be counted worthy of double honour, especially they who labour in the word and doctrine.

Just as the word liken occurs in Jacob 5 where the Lord says, "I will liken thee, O house of Israel, like unto a tame olive-tree" (Jacob 5:3), so many of the comparisons in biblical usage involving tree or plant imagery are also introduced by the words like, liken, and as. For example, Psalm 52:8, "I am like a green olive tree in the house of God"; Jeremiah 17:8, "He shall be as a tree planted by…
